toward the far edge of the settlement
around the back of the storehouse
along the path to the shrine
on the far side of the hill
through the marshes to the south
where the road meets the river
along the fence line to the west
at the bottom of the cliff trail
out toward the far grazing land
along the cart path east of here
across the bridge and up the hill
along the ridge to the northwest
down where the valley opens up
on the road to the next settlement
west, where the terrain gets rough
past the bend in the creek
near the forge on the east side
along the north bank of the river
out where the road turns to dirt
near the edge of the cultivated fields
through the gap in the western hills
up past where the avalanche came through
south of the last patrol marker
on the bluff overlooking the valley
near the root cellar behind the tavern
out where the mushrooms grow thick
at the edge of the peat bog
along the ridge where the hawks nest
near the spring on the hillside
toward the sun, about a half day's walk
down near the docks, if you can call them that
along the path the shepherds use
near the hollow where the mist collects
along the cliff face to the north
south, near where the ore was found
through the narrow pass to the east
at the top of the long staircase trail
near where the old road washed out
near the mouth of the cave system
where the trail crosses the ridgeline
along the base of the cliffs
out by the old ferry crossing
at the fork where the birch trees grow
along the edge of the marshland
up where the wild berries grow
near the old well at the crossroads
beyond the thicket on the western slope
along the path that hugs the river
past the place where lightning struck
